title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
summary: The Big Technology AI Summit convened leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and major media outlets on June 18, 2026, days after the U.S. government forced Anthropic to recall its frontier models Fable and Mythos. Discussions covered the Anthropic recall as a form of AI pause, infrastructure buildout and semiconductor supply chains, enterprise AI ROI metrics, cybersecurity implications, and competing visions of the compute-driven AI future.
writeup: This full session from Alex Kantrowitz's Big Technology AI Summit, recorded June 18, 2026, gathers leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and major media outlets, including Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, and Aaron Levie. The framing event is the U.S. government's forced recall of Anthropic's frontier models Fable and Mythos days earlier, which the panels treat as a de facto AI pause. Discussions cover infrastructure buildout and semiconductor supply chains, enterprise AI ROI and precision metrics, cybersecurity implications, and competing visions of a compute-driven future. It matters because it captures how industry figures interpret a government model recall and its consequences for frontier development.
